children.heading: Investigative jurisdiction over offenses against criminal laws
children.label: a Investigative jurisdiction over offenses against criminal laws
children.text: The Secretary shall have investigative jurisdiction over offenses against criminal laws of the United States in Indian country subject to an agreement between the Secretary and the Attorney General of the United States.

children.heading: Exercise of investigative authority
children.label: b Exercise of investigative authority
children.text: In exercising the investigative authority conferred by this section, the employees of the Bureau shall cooperate with the law enforcement agency having primary investigative jurisdiction over the offense committed.

children.heading: Law enforcement commission or other delegation of prior authority not invalidated or diminished
children.label: c Law enforcement commission or other delegation of prior authority not invalidated or diminished
children.text: This chapter does not invalidate or diminish any law enforcement commission or other delegation of authority issued under the authority of the Secretary before .August 18, 1990

children.heading: Authorities in addition to prior authority; civil or criminal jurisdiction, law enforcement, investigative, or judicial authority, of United States, Indian tribes, States, etc., unaffected
children.label: d Authorities in addition to prior authority; civil or criminal jurisdiction, law enforcement, investigative, or judicial authority, of United States, Indian tribes, States, etc., unaffected
children.text: The authority provided by this chapter is in addition to, and not in derogation of, any authority that existed before . The provisions of this chapter alter neither the civil or criminal jurisdiction of the United States, Indian tribes, States, or other political subdivisions or agencies, nor the law enforcement, investigative, or judicial authority of any Indian tribe, State, or political subdivision or agency thereof, or of any department, agency, court, or official of the United States other than the Secretary.August 18, 1990
